observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended the following to the
Management:

a) Through the MAO –
(cid:0) evaluate dormant or long inactive CIP accounts and draw a JEV to effect
the necessary journal entries to adjust said accounts to their appropriate
PPE accounts, and the necessary adjustments to correct equity account
relative to the depreciation of the reclassified PPE accounts;
(cid:0) adequately disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the Road
Network system;

b) Through the GSO –
(cid:0) observe the necessary protocols in the disposal of unserviceable
properties as stipulated in Joint Circular No. 2024-1 of COA and DBM;
(cid:0) to prepare, maintain and submit to the Municipal Accountant local road
network property cards to facilitate subsequent reconciliation with the
Report on Physical Count of Road Network System; and

c) For the MAO and the GSO - to ensure that the balances of the PPE accounts are
reconciled on a regular basis and that discrepancies noted shall be immediately
investigated and resolved to maintain the proper and true valuation of
accounts. Furthermore, both Offices need to collaborate their efforts and
practice regular coordination to warrant a more accurate report of PPE
balances.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended the following to the
Management:

a) Efficiently utilize the fund for the purpose designed and assess whether the
originally funded PPAs are feasible for implementation; otherwise, consider
reprogramming to more suitable PPAs to encompass the four (4) thematic areas
of DRRM and integrate them into the AIP for approval by the local Sanggunian
to serve as a roadmap in LDRRM Fund utilization and improve the capacity of
the Municipality to respond in emergencies or disasters;

b) Prioritize the timely submission of the monthly and annual Reports on Sources
and Utilization of the LDRRMF to the audit team and aforesaid government
oversight offices; and

c) Implement data verification and validation processes to ensure accurate
submission of the required documents related to the expenditure of various
DRRM PPAs.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended the following to the
Management:

a. Thoroughly review the required attachments embodied in government procurement
law and COA Circular 2009-001. A transmittal letter detailing the list of documents shall
be provided and the same be index-tabbed for easy-reference;

b. Submit the documents in its original form (two sets) as these are subject to review
and inspection by the COA Regional Office XII Technical Audit Group (TAG), when
warranted; and

c. Observe the timeline for submission of contracts, POs and supporting documents
for review, evaluation and inspection of projects, programs and activities (PPAs)
implemented by the Agency.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended the following to the
Management:

a) Plan properly the prioritization of PPAs to be funded by 20% DF to have a timely
implementation of the identified projects so that the purpose for which it was
undertaken can be maximized; and

b) Awareness of the issued COA Circular 2013-004 is important for compliance
with its reportorial and transparency requirements. Assign personnel, if
necessary, in updating the project status of projects and in facilitating the
maintenance of the list of all ongoing government PPAs.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended the following to the
Management:
a. For the Local Chief Executive, to direct the concerned department/personnel in
ensuring full implementation of the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances,
draw necessary accounting entries, measurements, presentation and disclosure
requirements as provided in the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020
and ensure completeness of details in the Report on the Physical Count of Property,
Plant, & Equipment (RPCPPE);
b. For the Inventory Committee to evaluate recorded items below the capitalization
criteria whether these are semi-expendable items misclassified as PPE which shall
have been expensed upon issuance or these are items with individual values below
threshold and reconcile RPCPPE to General Ledger and facilitate review of noted
variances;
For the Municipal Accountant to improve practices in reporting of accounts by
undertaking the following:
c. Properly observe the required five percent (5%) residual value across all classes of
items in the PPE for uniformity, unless there is sufficient reason for the LGU to apply
a different percentage in computing for the residual value of a particular item or class
of items in the PPE based on the nature of its operations;
d. draw a journal entry voucher to effect the necessary journal entries to adjust PPE
items relative to the required salvage value, the necessary adjustments to correct
equity account and reclassification of semi-expendable items with costs not
exceeding capitalization threshold upon coordination with the Inventory Committee; and
e. Maintain and update subsidiary records such as PPE ledger cards to properly
monitor the addition and disposal of PPEs.

We recommended the following to the management:
For the Accounting and the Treasury to:
a) Comply with the requirements on the conduct of monthly reconciliation
between Accounting and Treasury to lessen the accumulation of unreconciled items.
For the Municipal Accountant to:
b) Prepare the adjusting journal entry for the minor discrepancies mentioned in the
second observation above;
c) Conduct a detailed review of the bank reconciliation process and implement
measures to enhance accuracy and transparency in the amounts reported in the
financial statements. Strengthen the internal controls to help mitigate similar
discrepancies in the future; and
d) Perform the necessary actions and prepare the necessary journal entry to adjust the
amount of P202,727.63 in the Cash in Treasury balance and furnish the Audit Team
with the necessary records and documents to validate the adjustments, if any.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended the following to the
Management:
a) Efficiently utilize the fund for the purpose designed and assess whether the
originally funded PPAs are feasible for implementation; otherwise, consider
reprogramming to more suitable PPAs to encompass the four (4) thematic areas
of DRRM and integrate them into the AIP for approval by the local Sanggunian
to serve as a roadmap in LDRRM Fund utilization and improve the capacity of
the Municipality to respond in emergencies or disasters; and
b) Prepare a complete and comprehensive annual LDRRMFIP which shall include
the PPAs of current year appropriations and shall incorporate under a separate
caption the list of PPAs to be funded from the unexpended balances of previous
years LDRRMF and monitor the unexpended balances to be recognized under
the Trust Liabilities-DRRM account including its utilization and balances.

a.) For the Local Chief Executive to direct the concerned personnel in ensuring the full implementation of the one-time cleansing of PPE; b.) For the Inventory Committee to update the Physical Inventory Plan and ensure completion of the physical inventory-taking of all properties; c.) After which, require the immediate reconciliation with the accounting records to facilitate review of noted variances, effect necessary adjustments, provision of necessary disclosure in the notes to financial statements, and the necessary disposition for those non-existing/missing and disposal of unserviceable items; d.) For the inventory committee to adopt a uniform property identification system assigning property number indicated in tags for each PPE item; e.) For the Accountant and the GSO to prepare and update the PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively; f.) For the GSO to renew and update the Property Acknowledgement Receipts of all the equipment of the municipality; g.) Allocate in the annual budget the estimated cost for land titling to expedite the titling of lands acquired; h.) For the Accountant to record all lands acquired either by donation or purchase and have them register with the Registry of Deeds to secure their ownership; i.) For the GSO to insure all insurable properties to indemnify or compensate for any damage to, or loss of all its properties in case of destruction.
observations.managementAction: Issued office order in preparation of the one-time cleansing of PPE; Partially complied: update the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) and conduct physical inventory-taking of all properties; prepare and update PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards; renew and update PARs; requested system provider to update system to facilitate preparation of subsidiary ledgers; documents for land titling submitted to agencies like BIR and CENRO; GSO coordinated with GSIS on insurance; personnel assigned to focus on titling of lands.
observations.reason: The updated PIP was not yet submitted to the team. The preliminary activities were undertaken. Reconciliation of PPE balance is still on-going. Not all PPE balances were reconciled. Some PARs and ledger cards were updated; insufficient budget allocated for titling; waiting for supporting documents; documents for other items still in process.

observations.recommendation: a) Maintain subsidiary records for all funds received; and b) Coordinate with the Source Agencies to obtain a copy of the MOA or its equivalent documents for FT covering each project funded out of the fund transfers.
observations.managementAction: Complied accordingly: reclassified accounts not properly classified in the books; preparation of subsidiary records is ongoing; coordinated with source agencies requesting copies of MOA from source agencies for other projects.
observations.reason: Preparation of subsidiary records ongoing; balances from previous years not yet incorporated; coordination with source agencies is in process (requesting MOA copies).

observations.recommendation: To the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer: a) Retrieve any supporting documents, examine available records, and thoroughly analyze the accounts in order to determine/validate/verify/establish collectability and/or dormancy of the accounts. Furthermore, investigate any amounts that are not adequately supported by documentation. From then on, conduct periodic verification, analysis and validation of the existence of the receivable accounts, in accordance with Sections 6.1 and 7.1 of COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 to ensure that these are collected when these become due and demandable. Furthermore, coordinate in the preparation of the required schedules and aging on a quarterly basis; b) Prepare, maintain and submit the SLs and ensure that the details and significant information of the transactions are completely noted therein to facilitate the review of the correctness of the account balances, and ensure that these are regularly updated and reconciled to the GL. Moreover, establish proper safeguard thereof and the related significant files and documents of the Office; c) Accurately prepare the schedule of all receivables (including the unliquidated cash advances and fund transfers) in accordance with Section 6.2 of the said Circular, and the Aging of Dormant Receivable Accounts (as well as the dormant cash advances and inter-agency fund transfers, if any) on a quarterly basis as prescribed by Section 7.4 of the same Circular (Annexes 1 to 3) to support any request for write-off of the accounts; d) Prepare the necessary adjusting entry/ies to recognize any impairment in accordance with the IPSAS, any subsequent authorized write-off, and/or any recovery of the write-off vis-à-vis receivable accounts; To the Local Chief Executive: e) Direct the Management to exercise or exhaust all possible remedies for the collection of overdue accounts and if these efforts proved futile, document all actions undertaken to recover the accounts. Further, undertake necessary and appropriate actions prescribed in Section 8.2 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 with regard to filing a written request for authority to write-off the dormant accounts to the Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/or Supervising Auditor (SA) and the request be supported by: (1) a list of available records and extent of validation made on the accounts; (2) the supporting documents listed in Section 8.3 of the same circular; and (3) the aging of the dormant accounts
observations.managementAction: To the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer: a) Retrieve any supporting documents, examine available records, and thoroughly analyze the accounts in order to determine/validate/verify/establish collectability and/or dormancy of the accounts. Furthermore, investigate any amounts that are not adequately supported by documentation. From then on, conduct periodic verification, analysis and validation of the existence of the receivable accounts; b) Prepare, maintain and submit the SLs and ensure that the details and significant information of the transactions are completely noted therein to facilitate the review of the correctness of the account balances, and ensure that these are regularly updated and reconciled to the GL. Moreover, establish proper safeguard thereof and the related significant files and documents of the Office; c) Accurately prepare the schedule of all receivables (including the unliquidated cash advances and fund transfers) in accordance with Section 6.2 of the said Circular, and the Aging of Dormant Receivable Accounts on a quarterly basis; d) Prepare the necessary adjusting entry/ies to recognize any impairment in accordance with the IPSAS, any subsequent authorized write-off, and/or any recovery of the write-off vis-à-vis receivable accounts; e) Direct the Management to exercise or exhaust all possible remedies for the collection of overdue accounts and if these efforts proved futile, document all actions undertaken to recover the accounts and file written request for authority to write-off the dormant accounts with supporting documents.
observations.reason: No adequate documents on file; ongoing gathering of needed documents; Receivable to ONB due to overpayment of loans were noted; adjustments will be made after necessary documents are gathered.

observations.recommendation: To the LDRRMO, MPDC and MBO: a.) Set a specific and achievable implementation target period for each PPA of the LDRRMF in the LDRRMP and AIP; To the LDRRMO, MPDC, MBO, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant: b.) Collaborate to fast-track the transfer of the future unutilized/unexpended/unobligated balances of the current appropriations to the Special Trust Fund in order to hasten the accurate recording of such transfer both in General Fund and Trust Fund books before the year ends; and, To the LDRRMO: c.) Prioritize the timely submission of the monthly and annual Reports on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF to the audit team and aforesaid government oversight offices.
observations.managementAction: Complied: strictly utilize the 30% QRF in accordance with Section 22(c) of RA No. 10121 and Section 4.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-002; set implementation target periods; collaborated to fast-track transfers to Special Trust Fund; prioritized submission of reports though there was a delay in submission of the reports.
observations.reason: There was a delay in the submission of the monthly and annual reports; timely submission was prioritized but some reports were submitted late.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that management direct the Municipal Accountant to comply with the disclosure requirements of IPSAS by adequately providing a concise narrative on significant changes and breakdown of material balances to ensure that relevant information is available to the intended users of the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Complied: Details of the Loans Payable were disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements; Municipal Accountant prepared disclosures; Table for depreciation of PPEs was prepared.
observations.reason: Other required disclosures are still inadequate.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a) Specify the implementation target period of the projects in the Annual Investment Program to guide the Municipality in scheduling the procurement activities and evaluation of actual performance against standards on a periodic basis; and b) Require the MPDC to specify the target dates of project/s implementation and conduct regular evaluation of actual performance; conduct periodic monitoring and evaluation of PPAs and submit reports to the Mayor and audit team.
observations.managementAction: Actual monitoring schedules were done and directly reported to the LCE; MPDC to prepare the Procurement and Monitoring Report and submit reports; monitoring of PPAs is regularly conducted.
observations.reason: Most are still generic; hardcopy report prepared after inspection of PPAs; actual monitoring of PPAs is conducted regularly.

observations.recommendation: a.) provide justification for all variances in the GAD AR to facilitate proper evaluation of PPAs; b.) diligently prepare the GAD Accomplishment Report within the time frame required under the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2016-01; c.) update and institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered age- and sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation.
observations.managementAction: Complied: GAD AR was prepared; GAD Accomplishment Report prepared within required time frame; GAD database updated and institutionalized with adequate and systematically gathered age- and sex-disaggregated data.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal BAC to prepare and submit the Procurement Monitoring Report to the Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB) in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester, to identify and correct deviations from procurement standards.
observations.managementAction: Complied: Municipal's Bids and Awards Committee prepared the Procurement Monitoring Report and submitted same to the Government Procurement and Monitoring Board (GPPB) in printed and electronic format within the required period.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the regulation on the one-time cleansing of PPE, we recommended
that management create an Inventory Committee to come up with a Physical
Inventory Plan for a complete and thorough physical inventory of the
agency’s assets. After which, require the immediate reconciliation with the accounting
records to facilitate review of noted variances, effect necessary adjustments,
provision of necessary disclosure in the notes to financial statements, and the
necessary disposition for those non-existing/missing and disposal of
unserviceable items.

We also recommended that the following procedures be strictly observed:

a.) The Accounting Office and the General Services Office to:
• conduct periodic reconciliation of accounting and property records,
• maintain subsidiary records such as property cards and PPE ledger
cards,
• coordinate for the proper and uniform classification and recording of
PPEs;
b.) Insure all insurable properties to indemnify or compensate for any
damage to, or loss of all its properties in case of destruction; and
c.) Record all lands acquired either by donation or purchase and have
them register with the Registry of Deeds to secure their ownership.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to immediately draw Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to record the
amount that should be transferred to Special Trust Fund under “Trust Liability-
DRRM” account and adjust/correct the balances of the other affected accounts
in the Trust Fund books.

In view of the aforementioned observations, we recommended and the
management agreed to the following:

To the Municipal Accountant:
a. Prepare the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRSs) to
reconcile the Cash in Bank records for all of its bank accounts (whether
local or foreign) with the depository bank balances within ten (10) days
from receipt of the bank statements. Regularly and timely submit the
BRSs with complete supporting documents to the concerned COA
auditor for verification;
b. To be updated of any reconciling item and to identify the nature of the
undetermined reconciling items, conduct thorough analysis and review
of cash accounts through the Bank Statements, BRSs and its other
supporting documents as to ascertain the accuracy of the reports. The
book reconciling items noted in the BRSs that are validated by
supporting documents be immediately adjusted to correct the balances
of the Cash in Bank accounts;
c. Make the necessary adjusting entry for any stale-dated check to
exclude it from the list of outstanding checks, as follows:
Cash in Bank - Local Currency, Current Account ₱ XXXXX
Accounts Payable ₱ XXXXX
To the Municipal Treasurer or Disbursing Officer:
d. Notify the payees with stale checks in their possession for cancellation
and/or replacement and ensure that such cancellation and/or
replacement will be reported in the Accounting Office for timely and
appropriate adjustment/s in the books of accounts; and
To the Treasurer’s Office
e. Be familiarized with Section 82, Book 3 of the Updated Local Treasury
Operations Manual and Section 59, Chapter 3, Volume 1 of the Manual
on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government
Units (LGUs) regarding the cancellation process and the reporting of
cancelled checks and ensure that cancelled checks are reported and
attached to the RCI prepared at the period of cancellation and submit
the same to the audit team.

We reiterated our recommendation that management direct the Municipal
Accountant comply with the disclosure requirements of IPSAS by adequately
providing a concise narrative on significant changes and breakdown of
material balances to ensure that relevant information is available to the
intended users of the Financial Statements.

We recommended the following to the Management:

a. Resolve the variances in the utilization of LDRRMF that are reflected in
the Monthly Utilization Reports for CY 2021 and SAAOB for Current
Legislative Appropriations;
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the amount and details
of the utilized and unutilized balances of the current, continuing, and
previous years’ appropriations of the LDRRMF are accurately,
specifically, and adequately disclosed in the monthly LDRRMF
Utilization Reports using the prescribed format, in consonance with the
provisions of Sections 5.1.5 and 5.1.16 of COA Circular 2012-002; and
c. Direct the LDRRM Council/Officer to regularly submit the monthly and
annual Reports on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRM Fund to the
Office of the COA Auditor and to the respective government oversight
offices such as OCD, DILG, RDRRMC and DBM, in accordance with
Sections 5.1.5 and 5.1.6 of COA Circular 2012-002 and Section 7.0 of
NDRRMC, DBM, & DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-001.

We recommended that the LDRRMC, through its LDRRM Officer, prepare a
complete and comprehensive annual LDRRMF Investment Plan which shall
include the PPAs of current year appropriations and shall incorporate under a
separate caption the list of PPAs to be funded from the unexpended balances
of previous years’ LDRRMF.

We recommended that Management:
a.) Specify the implementation target period of the projects in the Annual
Investment Program to guide the Municipality in scheduling the
procurement activities and evaluation of actual performance against
standards on a periodic basis; and
b.) Require the MPDC to conduct periodic monitoring and evaluation of the
municipality's PPA implementation with appropriate reports submitted to
the Mayor for perusal and appropriate action and furnish the audit team of
the required reports for timely review and validation of accomplishments.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System-Technical Working Group
(GFPS-TWG):

a.) establish clear baselines and realistic estimates in budget preparation for PPAs
translated from the demands/commitments identified in the GAD agenda;
b.) provide justification for all variances in the GAD AR to facilitate proper
evaluation of PPAs;
c.) diligently prepare the GAD Accomplishment Report within the time frame as
required under the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No.
2016-01;
d.) update and institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and
systematically gathered age- and sex-disaggregated data which will
provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy
formulation

that the Municipal
Accounting Office, in
coordination with the
General Services
Office, reconcile the
municipality’s records
of properties and make
appropriate adjustments
in books to ascertain the
accuracy in the
presentation of the
Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
1 Section 259. Issuance of PPE. The property officer shall prepare the Property Acknowledgement Receipt to support the
issuance of property to end-user. The Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR) [previously Acknowledgement Receipt of
Equipment (ARE)] shall be renewed at least once in every three years or every time there is a change in accountable officer or
custodian of the property.
2
COA Circular 80-124 V.2. Property Identification and Coding - Government property shall be properly labelled and identified
by the Inventory Committee…
27
We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Accounting
Office, in coordination with the General Services Office, reconcile the records of the
municipality’s properties and make appropriate adjustments in books to ascertain the
accuracy in the presentation of the Financial Statements.

Recommendation:
We reiterated the following recommendation and the management committed to:
a. provide necessary appropriation to facilitate the processing of transfer of the land
titles in the Municipality; and
b. direct the General Services Officer to prioritize the titling of the untitled lands in
order to establish absolute ownership thereof.

Recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and the management agreed to direct
the Municipal Accountant to present the current portion of the Long-Term Loans as
of year-end under Loans Payables – Current Portion for purpose of fair presentation
in the Agency’s financial statements.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the management require:
a. the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to (i) comply with the
provisions of IPSAS 24 on the preparation of Statement of Comparison of Budget
and Actual Amounts; and (ii) reconcile their records on allotments and obligations
monthly so that any resulting discrepancies can be immediately verified and
corrected; and
b. the Municipal Accountant to provide disclosures on the face of the SCBAA and/or
in the Notes to FS the :(i) changes between the original and final budgets; (ii)
material discrepancies between the budget and actual amounts; and (iii)
reconciliation of actual amounts per SCBAA and per financial statements, to
enhance the transparency of the Municipality’s financial statements.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to
provide the lacking disclosures and to strictly adhere to the disclosure requirements
of IPSAS in the Notes to Financial Statements to ensure that the financial reports
effective understanding by the intended users.

Recommendation:
In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended the following and the
Management agreed to:
a. Require the Local Finance Committee (LFC) to timely and accurately prepare
the Monthly Reports on Fund Utilization and Status of Implementation of
PPAs for BGCM Fund using the prescribed format (Annex C), in compliance
with Section 4.2 of LBC No. 125;
b. Require the LFC to accomplish the necessary posting in their transparency
boards on a regular basis to always keep the public informed and updated on
the implementation and utilization of BGCM Fund pursuant to the
transparency and full disclosure policy of the government and Section 4.3 of
the said circular;
c. Send a written notice and submit all monthly reports for BGCM Fund
utilization to the DBM and other government oversight offices where the
submission of reports is required under existing laws, rules and regulations, in
compliance with Section 4.4 of the said circular;
d. Direct the LFC to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant concerning the
allotment, obligations and disbursements for accurate monthly reporting on
fund utilization; and
e. Require the LFC to convene in the determination of PPAs and expenses to be
sourced from the funds of the LGU in order to properly identify the specific
PPAs and to ensure that disbursements are only made within the limited
amounts and nature of the approved PPAs.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the management update their GAD Database and to
maximize the utilization of GAD funds through the implementation of GAD-related
programs and projects in order to attain the objective for which funds were provided.

We recommended that the management instruct the Municipal Accountant to carefully review the balances of the ledgers and to effect the adjustments for misclassified accounts. The Municipal Accountant is also required to submit supporting documents for the adjusting entry made crediting the Due to LGUs account.
observations.reason: Not yet done reviewing all Subsidiary Ledgers

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Inventory Committee, in coordination with the Municipal Accounting Office and the General Services Office, reconcile the records of the municipality’s properties and make appropriate adjustments in books to ascertain the accuracy in the presentation of the Financial Statements.
observations.reason: Not all inventory items were tagged and listed in the Inventory.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and the management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to present the current portion of the Long-Term Loans as of year-end under Loans Payables – Current Portion for purpose of fair presentation in the Agency’s financial statements.
observations.reason: Waiting for Statement of Account from MDFO.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our previous year’s recommendation that the management direct the General Services Officer to prioritize the titling of those untitled lands in the Municipality in order to establish absolute ownership thereof, and provide necessary appropriation to facilitate the processing of land titles of the Municipality to safeguard real properties and to document ownership over the said property.
observations.managementAction: Two (2) land titles were already posted.
observations.reason: Waiting for the necessary documents from the Seller

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management evaluate the receivable accounts presented as part of the LGU's current assets by determining whether collections will be realized within one year from the reporting date to enable correct presentation of accounts in the financial statements and require the Accountant to properly manage the subsidiary ledger, ensuring that the details of significant information of the transactions referred to are completely noted therein and ensure proper safeguard thereof and the related significant files and documents of the office.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to continue effective, efficient and aggressive tax collection effort and develop strategies to improve their collection efficiency. Aside from sending Notice of Delinquency, update the Provincial Treasurer on the matter of legal remedies for application of administrative and judicial actions to enforce collections from delinquent real property taxpayers.
observations.reason: Only few delinquent RPT accounts were collected.

observations.recommendation: a.) Department heads concerned should coordinate closely with responsible office/s in order to determine the status of those inter-agency transfers (NGAs, NGOs/POs, LGUs) still unliquidated and demand their respective terminal reports in order to effect prompt liquidation. b.) The Municipal Accountant should closely monitor compliance on the submission of liquidation documents within the prescribed period and improve the present monitoring and control measures on the liquidation of fund releases.
observations.managementAction: Liquidation of CFAD Projects reports were not properly evaluated.
observations.reason: Some of the liquidation reports were not yet properly evaluated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management ensure that all copies of approved contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents be submitted within five (5) working days from approval or issuance and within 24 hours from acceptance of deliveries for review, evaluation and inspection of items procured by the Agency.

We recommend that management require the Municipal's Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report to be approved by the head of the procuring entity and submit the same to the Government Procurement and Policy Board (GPPB) in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester.
observations.reason: Procurement Monitoring Report has not been uploaded in the GPPB website.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended that the management instruct the Municipal Accountant to
carefully review the balances of the ledgers and to effect the adjustments for misclassified
accounts. The Municipal Accountant is also required to submit supporting documents for
the adjusting entry made crediting the Due to LGUs account.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Inventory Committee, in coordination with the Municipal
Accounting Office and the General Services Office, reconcile the records of the
municipality’s properties and for the Municipal Accountant to make appropriate
adjustments in books to ascertain the accuracy in the presentation of the Financial
Statements. Further, the management is required to submit every year to the COA Auditor
the reconcile RCPPE not later than the 31st day of January of the ensuing year.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant present the current portion of the
Long-Term Loans as of year-end under Loans Payables – Current Portion in compliance
with PPSAS1 for purposes of fair presentation in the Agency’s financial statements.

Recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the management direct the General
Services Officer to prioritize the titling of those untitled lands in order to establish absolute
ownership thereof, and provide necessary appropriation to facilitate the processing of land
titles of lots of the Municipality to safeguard real properties and to document ownership
over the said properties.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the management evaluate the receivable accounts presented
as current assets by determining whether collections will be realized within one year from
the reporting date to enable correct presentation of accounts in the financial statements and
exhaust all remedies in collecting the receivable accounts.
Require the Accountant of the proper management of subsidiary ledger which is a
permanent accounting record, ensuring that the details of significant information of the
transactions referred to are completely noted therein and ensure proper safeguard thereof
and the related significant files and documents of the office.

We recommended that, the discrepancies noted in the Bank Reconciliation Statements should be adjusted immediately to correct the balances of the Cash in Bank Accounts. Moreover, to furnish Bank Reconciliation Statements to the bank especially if the identified bank error is for adjustment by the bank.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to make a thorough reconciliation of the books and the records of the Municipal Development Fund Office and the Land Bank of the Philippines in order to reflect the correct balances of the accounts and make the necessary adjusting entries to ensure the fair presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended that the General Services Officer be directed to prioritize the titling of those untitled lands in order to establish absolute ownership thereof, and for the Municipal Budget Officer to consider the funding requirement for the titling of lands in the budget preparation for the ensuing year.
observations.reason: Reiterated in report of Part II of the report

observations.recommendation: We recommend to direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare BRS as required by Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011; instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit BRS, including the paid checks and original copies of debit/credit memos, to the Audit Team within 10 days after the end of each month; advise the Municipal Accountant to always draw journal vouchers to record all valid reconciling items that require adjustment and corrections in the general or subsidiary ledgers; and adhere to the provision of COA Circular No. 96-011 in the preparation and submission of BRS.

We recommend that management: a. Require the Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of the annual physical count of all PPEs and generate a Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE). b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update the property, plant and equipment ledger cards in accordance with Section 120 of the same manual. In addition, reconcile the inventory report and the ledger cards and investigate any discrepancy noted. c. Require the Accounting and GSO to maintain ledger and property cards as control records to facilitate the regular reconciliation of records; and d. Make appropriate and necessary adjustments in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
a. Require the Accounting and General Services Office to maintain the PPE Ledger
Cards and Property Cards, respectively for each category of asset per fund, and
conduct periodic reconciliation of their respective records to forestall possible
discrepancies. Any discrepancy should be immediately investigated and properly
adjusted in the books to fairly present the said account in the financial
statements; and
b. Direct the GSO to update the Acknowledgement Receipts for Equipment
(ARE) as prescribed in Appendix 53 of the Manual on the NGAS to readily
identify the Accountable Officers of acquired government property.

Recommendations:
a. Department heads concerned should coordinate closely with responsible office/s
in order to determine the status of those inter-agency transfers (NGAs,
NGOs/POs, LGUs) still unliquidated and demand their respective terminal
reports in order to effect prompt liquidation.
b. The Municipal Accountant should closely monitor compliance on the submission
of liquidation documents within the prescribed period and improve the present
monitoring and control measures on the liquidation of fund releases.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer shall remit
promptly and intact all taxes withheld by the Municipality to the Bureau of Internal
Revenue in compliance with the BIR regulations.
We recommend to liquidate immediately the unsettled cash advances and strictly observe the provisions set forth in COA Circular Nos. 97-002.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the reciprocal accounts, prepare reconciliation statement, and effect adjustments to reflect the correct account balances, thus, present reliable data on the Municipality’s financial operation as of a given period of time.

We recommended discontinuance of the use of open dump sites and identify possible sanitary landfill to develop and operate as a final disposal site for solid wastes. This landfill shall be designed and operated in accordance with the guidelines/ criteria mentioned in the said act.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management exert their best effort to fully accomplish the targeted priority development projects and programs on its mandate. The Local Chief Executive must see to it that the 20% of IRA be optimally utilized to help attain desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes. Strictly administer and supervise the implementation of development projects and likewise monitor its implementation.
observations.reason: Implementation of projects for 20% Development Fund are still on going

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management intensify efforts in the establishment and maintenance of a GAD database containing all sex-disaggregated data and gender statistics, in compliance with the PCW/NEDA/DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01 and to ensure that the GAD Focal Person should undergo trainings related to GAD to better equip her on the task at hand.

Management: a. Ensure completeness of the supporting documents of all contracts/ purchase orders submitted for review pursuant to COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009. b. Require the personnel in-charge in the submission of the contracts/ purchase orders and its supporting documents to maintain a checklist of required documents for each of the transactions to serve as a guide to ensure the completeness of the documents submitted to COA for review.
We recommend that Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant not to process claims without complete documentation and all the requisites of the claims are properly complied with.

We recommend that management require the Municipal's Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report to be approved by the head of the procuring entity and submit the same to the Government Procurement and Policy Board (GPPB) in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester.
observations.reason: Procurement Monitoring Report has not yet been submitted for 2nd semester.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Accounting Department should establish the depreciation of the PPE accounts and make the necessary adjusting entries to take up the depreciation expenses including the accumulated depreciation for prior years.

We recommend management open a separate bank account as special trust fund for the unexpended balance of LDRRMF for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities of LDRRMCs within the next five (5) years.

We recommend to direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare BRS as required by Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011; instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit BRS, including the paid checks and the original copies of debit/credit memos, to the Audit Team within 10 days after the end of each month; advise the Municipal Accountant to always draw journal vouchers to record all valid reconciling items that require adjustment and corrections in the general or subsidiary ledgers; and adhere to the provision of COA Circular No. 96-011 in the preparation and submission of BRS.
observations.reason: The depository bank is late in submitted bank statements to the Municipality

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:
a. Require the Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of the annual physical
count of all PPEs and inventories and generate a Report on the Physical Count of
Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update the property, plant and
equipment ledger cards in accordance with Section 120 of the same manual. In
addition, reconcile the inventory report and the ledger cards and investigate any
discrepancy noted.
c. Require the Accounting and GSO to maintain ledger and property cards as control
records to facilitate the regular reconciliation of records; and
d. Make appropriate and necessary adjustments in the books of accounts.

We recommend to liquidate immediately the unsettled cash advances and strictly
observe the provisions set forth in COA Circular Nos. 97-002.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer shall remit
promptly and intact all taxes withheld by the Municipality to the Bureau of Internal
Revenue in compliance with the BIR regulations.

We recommend that management direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the
reciprocal accounts, prepare reconciliation statement, and effect adjustments to reflect the
correct account balances, thus, present reliable data on the Municipality’s financial
operation as of a given period of time.

We recommend discontinuance of the use of open dump sites and identify possible
sanitary landfill to develop and operate as a final disposal site for solid wastes. This landfill
shall be designed and operated in accordance with the guidelines/criteria mentioned in the
said act.

We recommend that the management ensure that all copies of approved contracts,
purchase orders and their supporting documents be submitted within five (5) working days
from approval or issuance and within 24 hours from acceptance of deliveries for review,
evaluation and inspection of items procured by the Agency.

We recommend that the Municipal Health Office coordinate with the barangay
health centers and determine their needs that can be funded by the PFPR so that the
government’s goal of providing preventive care services to indigents will be fully
implemented, and thus, best results could be achieved in the most opportune time.

We recommend that the management exert their best effort to fully accomplish the
targeted priority development projects and programs embodied in the Annual Investment
Plan pursuant to its mandate.
The Local Chief Executive must see to it that the 20% of IRA be optimally utilized
to help attain desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes. Strictly
administer and supervise the implementation of development projects and likewise
monitor its implementation.

We recommend that Management intensify efforts in the establishment and
maintenance of a GAD database containing all sex-disaggregated data and gender statistics,
in compliance with the PCW/NEDA/DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01 and to ensure that the
GAD Focal Person should undergo trainings related to GAD to better equip her on the task
at hand.

We recommend that Management:
a. Ensure completeness of the supporting documents of all contracts submitted
for review pursuant to COA Circular 2009-001 dated Feb. 12, 2009.
b. Require the personnel in-charge in the submission of the contracts and its
supporting documents to maintain a checklist of required documents for each
of the transactions to serve as a guide to ensure the completeness of the
documents submitted to COA for review.

We recommend to the General Services Office to regularly monitor the expiration of all motor vehicles registration and renewal/registration to avoid invalidation and fines/penalties for late/non-registration.
We recommend that Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant not to process claims without complete documentation and all the requisites of the claims are properly complied with.

observations.reason: The said report was prepared but not submitted and uploaded to the Municipal's Bids and Awards

observations.reason: The DRRM Fund unexpended balance is still not transferred

observations.recommendation: We recommend to direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare BRS as required by Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011; instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit BRS, including the paid checks and original copies of debit/credit memos, to the Audit Team within 10 days after the end of each month; advise the Municipal Accountant to always draw journal vouchers to record all valid reconciling items that require adjustment and corrections in the general or subsidiary ledgers; and adhere to the provision of COA Circular No. 96-011 in the preparation and submission of BRS.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management exert their best effort to fully accomplish the targeted development projects and programs under the 20% Local Development Fund. Determine the reason for non-implementation of some projects and evaluate the possibility to make amendments to some projects which are not feasible for implementation.
observations.reason: There were projects that is still to be implemented. The ensuing year’s percentage of completion of projects are still low.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management discontinue the practice of allowing deductions on monthly loan amortizations that would result to a net pay below P3,000.00. The personnel who contracted loans from private lending establishments must directly remit their payments to the collecting agents/office of these creditors.
